title: Velvety Herbal Tripe Stir-fry
description: A nutritious and comforting brunch dish featuring tripe marinated in a herbal sauce for a velvety texture that complements the Malaysian flair.
instructions: Rinse the tripe under cold water and soak in vegetable broth for at least 30 minutes to remove any unpleasant odour.
Peel and roughly chop the fresh herbs, garlic cloves, and red chillies. Sauté them in a pan with oil until they are fragrant and slightly browned.
Add the soaked tripe to the sautéed herb mixture. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Transfer the tripe into a blender along with vegetable broth and blend well until smooth, adding more broth if needed for desired consistency.
In a wok or large skillet, heat another tablespoon of oil over medium-high heat. Add the blended herbal soup to the pan, stirring continuously to prevent sticking.
Cook for about 3-5 minutes until the soup thickens and the tripe is nicely glazed with the sauce.
Serve hot as a side dish with steamed rice or noodles.
